notes: Roman figure known for destroying prominent men of Gabii and distributing their wealth; mentioned in Cicero's letters and Cassius Dio's history.

docs.title: Meditations, Book 1
docs.author: Marcus Aurelius
docs.excerpt: Dignity without pretence, solicitous consideration for friends, tolerance of amateurs and of those whose opinions have no ground in science. A happy accommodation to every man, so that not only was his conversation more agreeable than any flattery, but he excited the greatest
